data.title: Threat Canvas
data.description: Click any threat actor to focus their attack vectors on the asset ring.

data.title: Guard Vectors
data.description: Select a guard cell to visualize the attack vector and smart contract mitigation.

guards.name: Unauthorized enode registration
guards.attack: An unregistered device attempts to map itself to a consensus node without prior authorization.
guards.guard: Device-to-node mapping requires prior consensus-layer authorization; the registry rejects linkage without it.

guards.name: Non-manager role impersonation
guards.attack: A random account attempts to call a manager-restricted consensus function.
guards.guard: RBAC modifier checks the caller's manager flag on-chain and reverts if absent.

guards.name: Manager delegation abuse
guards.attack: A manager attempts to appoint another manager, bypassing owner authority.
guards.guard: Manager appointment is restricted to the contract owner; only the owner can grant or revoke manager status.

guards.name: Owner self-ban
guards.attack: An attacker with registry access tries to ban the contract owner address, locking the system.
guards.guard: Ban operation reverts when the target address is the contract owner.

guards.name: Administrative zombie
guards.attack: A banned manager's address persists in the consensus layer and executes operations.
guards.guard: Cross-contract isolation: consensus layer queries registry ban status before authorizing any operation.

guards.name: Pending owner griefing
guards.attack: During a two-step ownership transfer, the current owner tries to ban the pending recipient.
guards.guard: Ban reverts when the target is a pending ownership transfer recipient.

guards.name: Mapping overwrite (1:1 violation)
guards.attack: An attacker remaps Device A to a different node, or remaps two devices to the same node.
guards.guard: Bijective mapping enforced; each device maps to exactly one node and vice versa. Both remap attempts revert.

guards.name: Unlinked device bypass
guards.attack: A device that is not yet linked to a node tries to receive enrollment or record capabilities.
guards.guard: Authority grants require prior device-to-node linkage. Unregistered devices cannot receive capability flags.

guards.name: Unauthorized credential reissuance
guards.attack: A caller without canReissue flag submits a reissuance TX, even with a valid consent voucher.
guards.guard: Reissuance checks canReissue[C] first; unauthorized callers are rejected regardless of voucher validity.

guards.name: Outbound dialing to banned node
guards.attack: A compromised honest node tries to connect outbound to a banned validator, leaking gossip.
guards.guard: Connection authorization check rejects outbound connections to banned node hashes before any handshake.

guards.name: Suspension bypass / duplicate
guards.attack: An attacker submits a duplicate suspension or attempts suspension on an already-suspended DID.
guards.guard: Suspension checks active enrollment status; duplicate suspension reverts. Reinstatement is a separate guarded function.

guards.name: Consent-less reissuance
guards.attack: An admin attempts to reissue a user's credential without any signed consent from the holder.
guards.guard: Reissuance requires a valid EIP-712 REISSUE_CONSENT voucher; no consent, no reissuance, regardless of role.

guards.name: Forged consent signature
guards.attack: A voucher is signed by a different account's key (wrong signer) and submitted for reissuance.
guards.guard: ECDSA recovery validates the recovered signer against the user's registered DID; mismatch causes revert.

guards.name: Voucher replay (consumed nonce)
guards.attack: A previously consumed consent voucher is resubmitted after the first successful reissuance.
guards.guard: Per-user nonce increments atomically on each use; stale nonces are rejected immediately.

guards.name: Suspended user reissuance
guards.attack: A valid consent voucher is submitted for a user who is currently suspended.
guards.guard: Reissuance checks suspension status before processing; suspended users cannot be reissued.

guards.name: Premature revocation finalization
guards.attack: A manager tries to finalize a revocation immediately after requesting it, bypassing the grace period.
guards.guard: Finalization enforces a configurable grace period via block-timestamp check; early finalization reverts.

guards.name: Reissuance during pending revocation
guards.attack: A reissuance TX is submitted while a revocation is already pending for the same user.
guards.guard: Reissuance checks for active revocationPending flag; blocked to prevent race-condition abuse.

guards.name: Nonce double-spend
guards.attack: Two transactions bearing the same voucher nonce are submitted in rapid succession.
guards.guard: Nonce is consumed on first use; the identical second voucher reverts because expected nonce has already incremented.

guards.name: Manager deadlock
guards.attack: All explicit managers are removed, leaving no one with manager-restricted capabilities.
guards.guard: Owner retains fallback manager privileges regardless of explicit manager state — deadlock is structurally impossible.

guards.name: Cross-contract state desync
guards.attack: A device is removed from the consensus layer only, leaving its permissions orphaned in Permissions.sol.
guards.guard: Atomic revocation clears device mapping and strips all associated permissions in a single transaction.

guards.name: Signature malleability (s-value)
guards.attack: A non-canonical s-value flip is applied to an otherwise valid EIP-712 signature to forge a new valid-looking sig.
guards.guard: Signature recovery rejects non-canonical s-values (s > secp256k1n/2), enforcing strict EIP-2 compliance.

guards.name: Malformed payload bloat
guards.attack: A 50 KB junk DID or CID payload is submitted to enrollment, attempting a gas exhaustion attack.
guards.guard: Oversized payload exceeds block gas limit; transaction reverts before any state mutation occurs.

data.title: Temporal Vulnerability Analysis
data.description: Drag the timeline cursor to scrub through lifecycle phases and inspect vulnerability windows.

vulnerabilities.text: K1: Key generation entropy; CSPRNG quality determines root key security
Admin registration error could trigger G1/G8 guard failures
K5: owner_key transmission; requires secure out-of-band channel
Swarm key must be distributed before IPFS sidecar starts
K1: Disk theft possible; mitigated by TPM binding
N1: Network scanning; mitigated by firewall and TLS
I4: Swarm key risk if kiosk is physically accessed
K3: master_seed in RAM during derivation; no TEE protection in current prototype
K4: RAM dump outside derivation window has no impact (memory cleared immediately)
Window is ~10ms; material is cleared post-derivation regardless
Bio1: Presentation attack; mitigated by CFPAD liveness detection
N3: Verification interception; mitigated by TLS and ephemeral session signature
K5: Theft-to-revocation gap; DIDs can be enumerated during this window
Delayed admin notification extends the attacker's effective window
P5: TX flooding may escalate before device auto-suspend triggers
Contract-based sync provides near-instant cross-node revocation
G16/G17: Grace period and pending-revocation guards enforced on-chain
Re-provisioning must use new entropy; reuse of compromised salt creates linkable templates
G12: Re-enrollment requires biometric presence at active kiosk
Data remnants on storage medium; secure wipe protocol required
Node must be removed from Permissions.sol before physical decommission
Attacker gains full physical access to kiosk hardware
All file-based keys readable if no TPM binding (device_root_key, prism_wallet.key)
Blockchain wallet key exposed; attacker can sign transactions as the kiosk
K1: Disk contents fully readable; all file-based key material extracted
K5: Attacker queries blockchain using stolen wallet key, enumerating enrolled DIDs
K8: IPFS swarm accessible; encrypted vaults can be exfiltrated (ciphertext, not plaintext)
K6: If 2+ kiosks stolen simultaneously, QBFT threshold (f=1) may be breached
Admin calls suspendDevice(); contract updated, kiosk blocked on-chain
G20: Cross-contract desync must be checked and atomically remediated
Stolen kiosk cannot submit TXs or participate in consensus
Encrypted vault ciphertexts on stolen device remain opaque; no biometric = no decryption
Affected users must re-enroll at an active kiosk to recover access
User presents biometrics at active kiosk with new random salt
New salt produces orthogonal key; all stolen material is mathematically useless
New DID issued on-chain; previous credential permanently invalidated
All credentials rotated; attacker's stolen material has zero residual utility
Post-incident: review IPFS exfiltration logs and validator consensus health metrics
